top_stories.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
top_stories.summary: Microsoft patched a flaw in the Microsoft Entra ID "Agent ID Administrator" role that allowed privilege escalation and service principal takeover attacks. Silverfort researchers discovered the vulnerability in the built-in role, which was introduced to manage AI agent identity lifecycle operations within Entra ID.

top_stories.title: Microsoft fixes Entra ID flaw enabling privilege escalation
top_stories.summary: Microsoft patched a privilege escalation vulnerability in Microsoft Entra ID where the Agent ID Administrator role could be exploited to enable account takeover of service accounts. The flaw allowed attackers with access to that role to escalate privileges beyond their intended permissions within the Entra ID identity platform.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra Agent ID Flaw Enabled Tenant Takeover via Privilege Escalation
top_stories.summary: A security flaw in Microsoft Entra Agent ID allowed attackers to escalate privileges and achieve full tenant takeover through Service Principal abuse. Microsoft has fully patched the vulnerability.

top_stories.title: Microsoft to roll out Entra passkeys on Windows in late April
top_stories.summary: Microsoft will begin rolling out passkey support for phishing-resistant passwordless authentication to Microsoft Entra-protected resources from Windows devices starting late April. The update enables passwordless authentication for Entra-managed environments directly from Windows devices.

top_stories.title: Learning from the Vercel breach: Shadow AI & OAuth sprawl
top_stories.summary: The Vercel breach demonstrated how a compromised third-party OAuth integration can provide attackers direct access to downstream customer environments. The incident highlights risks associated with OAuth sprawl and shadow AI integrations as attack vectors within identity and access management frameworks.

news.title: Official SAP npm packages compromised to steal credentials
news.summary: Multiple official SAP npm packages were compromised in a supply-chain attack attributed to TeamPCP, targeting developers' systems. The attack was designed to steal credentials and authentication tokens from affected development environments.

news.title: SAP-Related npm Packages Compromised in Credential-Stealing Supply Chain Attack
news.summary: A supply chain attack campaign dubbed "mini Shai-Hulud" has compromised SAP-related npm packages with credential-stealing malware. The attack was identified by multiple cybersecurity firms including Aikido Security, SafeDep, Socket, StepSecurity, and Wiz, targeting packages associated with SAP's JavaScript and cloud application ecosystem.

news.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
news.summary: A webinar is being promoted around the threat of AI-automated attacks targeting Active Directory and Domain Admin credentials. The session focuses on autonomous agents capable of mapping Active Directory and escalating privileges rapidly, framed around exposure validation workflows.

news.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
news.summary: cPanel has released security updates addressing a critical authentication vulnerability affecting all supported versions of cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM). The flaw impacts multiple authentication paths and could allow attackers to gain unauthorized access to the control panel software.

news.title: Why Secure Data Movement Is the Zero Trust Bottleneck Nobody Talks About
news.summary: New research from an unnamed team surveyed 500 security professionals and identified secure data movement as a bottleneck in Zero Trust program implementation. The report, titled "Cyber360: Defending the Digital Battlespace," argues that assumptions about connectivity undermine Zero Trust deployments.

news.title: Access control with IAM Identity Center session tags
news.summary: AWS IAM Identity Center supports session tags as a mechanism for implementing attribute-based access control (ABAC) across multi-account AWS environments. The feature enables organizations to manage workforce access centrally by passing user attributes as session tags to enforce fine-grained authorization policies at scale.

news.title: Why AI agents are triggering a rethink of enterprise identity
news.summary: Computer Weekly's Security Think Tank examines how agentic AI is impacting enterprise identity and access management. The article analyzes the specific security stack changes required as AI agents introduce new challenges for traditional IAM frameworks.

news.title: Silverfort acquires Fabrix Security to bring AI decision-making to runtime access control
news.summary: Silverfort has acquired Fabrix Security, an AI-native identity security startup founded in 2024, for an undisclosed sum. The acquisition is aimed at integrating AI-driven decision-making capabilities into runtime access control within Silverfort's unified identity security platform.

news.title: Simplifying AWS defense with Microsoft Sentinel UEBA
news.summary: Microsoft Sentinel's User and Entity Behavior Analytics (UEBA) capability is being applied to AWS CloudTrail log analysis to differentiate legitimate user activity from potential attacker behavior. The approach uses behavioral baselines derived from user, peer, and device patterns to generate binary signals that enrich raw cloud access logs for defenders.

news.title: Five-minute interview: Zair Abbas, Okta
news.summary: Okta's partner ecosystem lead for EMEA, Zair Abbas, participated in a brief personal interview format. The article is a short profile piece focused on personal insights rather than substantive IAM product or business news.

news.title: How to fix cybersecurity's agentic AI identity crisis
news.summary: The article addresses security challenges posed by AI agents in enterprise environments, focusing on identity and access management for autonomous AI systems. The piece covers how agentic AI creates new requirements for authentication, authorization, and identity governance frameworks.

news.title: Can I do that with policy? Understanding the AWS Service Authorization Reference
news.summary: AWS published guidance explaining the capabilities and limitations of AWS IAM policies, covering service control policies (SCPs) and AWS Organizations. The article addresses how IAM policies interact with AWS services to help practitioners build effective authorization controls.

news.title: Why AI is forcing a reset of the identity stack
news.summary: Computer Weekly's Security Think Tank examines how AI is driving changes in identity and access management practices. The piece focuses on the concept of shifting from traditional identity management toward AI-driven "identity intelligence."

news.title: Bridging the AI Agent Authority Gap: Continuous Observability as the Decision Engine
news.summary: The article examines the "AI Agent Authority Gap" in enterprise security, focusing on how AI agents operate as delegated actors requiring governance frameworks. The piece addresses authorization and delegation challenges posed by AI agents within identity and access management contexts.

news.title: DORA and operational resilience: Credential management as a financial risk control
news.summary: The article examines how DORA's Article 9 establishes authentication and access control as legal obligations for EU financial entities. It covers the regulation's specific requirements around credential management and the consequences of control failures in a financial services context.

news.title: The New World ID: Proof of Human for the AI Era Scales Across the Digital Platforms People and Businesses Use Every Day
news.summary: World announced the next generation of World ID, a proof-of-human protocol, with Okta among the partners integrating it alongside Docusign, Tinder, Vercel, and Zoom. The protocol is designed to verify human identity versus AI agents across digital platforms, positioning itself as an identity layer for the AI era.

news.title: 'Identity is the new battleground': Why your IT helpdesk is suddenly getting a lot of bizarre calls
news.summary: Okta-targeted vishing attacks are exploiting IT helpdesks through social engineering, using impersonation and manufactured urgency to manipulate support staff. These attacks bypass technical controls by targeting human operators, enabling attackers to escalate privileges and trigger large-scale data breaches.

news.title: Regular Password Resets Aren’t as Safe as You Think
news.summary: Specops Software has published research showing how regular password resets can be exploited by attackers through helpdesk social engineering to achieve full account compromise. The research highlights how reset workflows can bypass security controls when helpdesk staff are manipulated into processing fraudulent reset requests.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted to conducting SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access.
